evals.prompt: I have a JSONL file with chat conversations. Help me write a script to fine-tune Llama-3.1-8B on it.
evals.expected_output: Complete runnable SFT script using FromConversationFileBuilder with ChatDatasetBuilderCommonConfig
expectations: Uses FromConversationFileBuilder from tinker_cookbook.supervised.data
Uses ChatDatasetBuilderCommonConfig with correct fields (model_name_for_tokenizer, renderer_name, max_length, batch_size, train_on_what)
Uses model_info.get_recommended_renderer_name() for renderer — not hardcoded
Includes TrainOnWhat.ALL_ASSISTANT_MESSAGES or explains the TrainOnWhat options
Uses cli_utils.check_log_dir() before training
The script is complete and runnable (has if __name__ == '__main__' block or equivalent)
Uses hyperparam_utils.get_lr() or explains that LoRA LR should be ~10x higher than full FT
Explains that batch_size is measured in tokens, not examples
Mentions the expected JSONL format: each line has {messages: [{role, content}, ...]}

evals.prompt: I want to distill knowledge from Qwen3-8B into Qwen3-8B-Base. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: On-policy distillation setup using TeacherConfig, PromptOnlyDatasetBuilder, and DistillationDatasetConfig
expectations: Uses TeacherConfig with the teacher model (Qwen3-8B)
Uses PromptOnlyDatasetBuilder with a dataset_name like 'deepmath' or 'tulu3'
Uses DistillationDatasetConfig to bind the dataset to the teacher
Uses train_on_policy.Config or the distillation training entry point
Mentions kl_penalty_coef as the key supervision signal
Sets the student model to Qwen3-8B-Base
Explains the difference between on-policy (recommended) and off-policy distillation
Mentions multi-teacher as an option for combining datasets

evals.prompt: What renderers are available for Qwen models and how do I handle vision inputs?
evals.expected_output: List of Qwen renderers with vision handling using image content parts
expectations: Lists qwen3, qwen3_disable_thinking, qwen3_vl, qwen3_5 and related variants
Shows the image content part format with type='image' and image_url
Mentions using a VL renderer for vision inputs (qwen3_vl or qwen3_5)
Uses model_info.get_recommended_renderer_name() rather than manual selection
Explains that thinking-enabled vs disabled renderers exist and when to use each
Mentions the custom renderer registration API (register_renderer) as an option
